{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation on delivery changes, vehicle class enforcement, and proactive customer notification. The feature spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet management, delivery lifecycle, and customer communication.",
    "bounded_context": "Four bounded contexts identified: (1) Route Optimization — owns route calculation, recalculation triggers, stop time estimates; (2) Fleet & Driver Management — owns driver shifts, vehicle licensing, zone assignments; (3) Delivery Management — owns delivery lifecycle, zone allocation, delivery status transitions; (4) Customer Notification — owns notification rules, 'next N stops' window tracking.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Optimized Route", "definition": "An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize travel time/distance within a zone for pending deliveries matching the driver's vehicle class." },

      { "term": "Shift Start", "definition": "The event when a driver begins their active duty period, triggering initial route assignment." },
      { "term": "Route Recalculation", "definition": "A re-optimization of the driver's remaining stops triggered by a new delivery in zone, a delivery failure, or a manual route deviation." },
      { "term": "Licensed Vehicle Class", "definition": "The category of vehicle a driver is authorized to operate (e.g., motorcycle, van, freight truck), which constrains which deliveries they may be assigned." },
      { "term": "Next 3 Stops Window", "definition": "A rolling lookahead of the three upcoming deliveries on a driver's active route. Entering this window triggers customer notification." },
      { "term": "Stop Time Estimate", "definition": "The estimated duration a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes but is overridable per zone by the operations team." },
      { "term": "Manual Deviation", "definition": "When a driver physically departs from the suggested route sequence, detected by the system (definition of deviation threshold is unspecified)." },
      { "term": "Pending Delivery", "definition": "A delivery that is awaiting assignment to a driver route — not yet in-transit, completed, or failed." },
      { "term": "Failed Delivery", "definition": "A delivery where the driver attempted but could not complete the handoff (e.g., customer absent, access denied), removing it from the active route and triggering recalculation." }
